notes: Jersey should score English as the practical everyday language for travelers while recognizing Jerriais as a protected heritage language and French as historically significant.

evidence.title: Government of Jersey: Improve your English
evidence.note: Government guidance says Jersey is predominantly English speaking, used for government services, businesses, and the majority of signs.

evidence.title: Government of Jersey: Jerriais traditional language
evidence.note: Government guidance describes Jerriais as Jersey's traditional language and confirms it is not widely spoken today.

notes: Guernsey should score English as the main traveler language while recognizing Guernesiais and French as culturally important but lower-coverage today.

evidence.title: Visit Guernsey: Which languages are spoken in Guernsey?
evidence.note: Official tourism content says English is Guernsey's main language today and explains the heritage role of Guernesiais and historical French.

evidence.title: Visit Guernsey: multilingual guide availability
evidence.note: Official tourism guidance shows English by default and notes French-language guide availability.

notes: The Isle of Man should score English as fully usable for travelers while recognizing Manx Gaelic as a heritage language with signage and cultural visibility.

evidence.title: Isle of Man visitor guide: language
evidence.note: Official visitor guidance says English is the spoken and written language of the Isle of Man, while Manx Gaelic is the traditional language.

evidence.title: Isle of Man visitor guide basics
evidence.note: Official visitor basics list the island's language as English.

notes: Hong Kong should recognize both English and Chinese as official while acknowledging Cantonese as the dominant spoken variety in daily life.

evidence.title: Hong Kong Tourism Board: Good to know
evidence.note: Official tourism guidance says Chinese and English are the official languages and that Cantonese is the mother tongue of the majority of residents.

evidence.title: Hong Kong Official Languages Ordinance
evidence.note: Hong Kong law gives Chinese and English equal official status.

notes: Macao should recognize Chinese and Portuguese as official while giving English meaningful credit for tourism and commerce. Cantonese is the dominant spoken Chinese variety in daily life.

evidence.title: Macao Government Tourism Office: Language
evidence.note: Official tourism guidance says Chinese and Portuguese are official, Cantonese is the most widely spoken, and English is generally used in trade, tourism and commerce.

evidence.title: Macao SAR Government Portal tourism language note
evidence.note: The Macao SAR government portal publishes official tourism information in Chinese, Portuguese, and English.
